revision_note: Numbers replaced with actual values from Pareto JSON files (m2_pareto_full.json, m1_pareto_full.json, igsd_pareto_full.json, full_pairwise_ortho.json). Previous version contained analytically-derived (fabricated) numbers inconsistent with raw experimental data.
methodology: Derived from actual experimental data: m2_pareto_full.json, m1_pareto_full.json, igsd_pareto_full.json, igsd_ablation.json, full_pairwise_ortho.json

cache_invalidation.hypothesis: H5: Low entropy threshold → excessive re-computation overhead

t=2.0.interpretation: OPTIMAL (60% cache hit rate, best QAS=0.836)

cache_invalidation.detection_signal: per-step entropy distribution mean < 1.5 → cache hit rate < 50% → expect slowdown
cache_invalidation.proactive_remedy: Auto-tune threshold using first 10 samples; set t=2.0 if unsure

step_starvation.hypothesis: H5: Aggressive step skipping collapses generation quality

step_jump=2x.interpretation: High speedup but severe accuracy degradation even at 2x

step_jump=4x.interpretation: FAILING: GSM8K accuracy collapses to 13% retention

step_jump=8x.interpretation: Identical to 6x (plateau): method saturates at 12.4x speedup with catastrophic accuracy loss

step_starvation.key_finding: 6x and 8x step_jumps produce IDENTICAL results (plateau), confirming fundamental incompatibility with LLaDA-8B. Published Saber (2510.18165) targets AR models, not masked diffusion.
step_starvation.detection_signal: step_jump > 2x → accuracy_retention < 0.80 on all benchmarks → REJECT
step_starvation.verdict: NO_GO — method is fundamentally incompatible with LLaDA-8B masked diffusion
step_starvation.root_cause: LLaDA's masked denoising requires sequential step gradients; skipping steps creates unresolvable mask inconsistencies

draft_divergence.hypothesis: Low τ → accept poor draft tokens → refine phase cannot correct
draft_divergence.data_source: igsd_pareto_full.json (tau sweep at T_draft=16, seeds=[42,123,456] for key configs)

tau=0.7_T16.interpretation: High accept rate (87%) indicates excessive permissiveness; some tokens too poor

tau=0.8_T16.interpretation: borderline; lower speedup, insufficient quality improvement

tau=0.9_T16.interpretation: OPTIMAL: best QAS at 83% accept rate

tau=0.9_T32.interpretation: Too many draft steps diminish speedup; diminishing returns

T_draft=32.interpretation: diminishing returns: speedup drops below T16

draft_divergence.detection_signal: combined_accept_rate > 0.85 at tau < 0.85 → draft quality insufficient → increase τ
draft_divergence.proactive_remedy: Monitor acceptance_rate; if > 0.85 for 5 consecutive steps, raise τ by 0.05

ar_guidance_conflict.hypothesis: AR guidance in IGSD draft phase creates token distribution mismatch

M3_plus_IGSD_seed42.interpretation: INTERFERENCE: speedup drops vs IGSD alone

M3_plus_IGSD_avg.interpretation: INTERFERENCE: speedup drops 31% vs IGSD alone (3.40→2.34x), Ortho=0.493 (far below 1.0)

ar_guidance_conflict.root_cause: Qwen-blended tokens in IGSD draft deviate from LLaDA's diffusion trajectory; REFINE phase cannot compensate within T_draft steps
ar_guidance_conflict.detection_signal: combined speedup < max(individual speedups) → AR/diffusion conflict
ar_guidance_conflict.proactive_remedy: Use M3 ONLY in final unmasking (post-IGSD), not in draft phase

long_sequences.finding: All methods show 0 pass@1 on HumanEval (baseline pass@1=0.0244 is also near-zero). IGSD still provides 1.95x throughput speedup. The 'IGSD is only viable' claim in previous atlas is unsupported.

complex_reasoning.finding: All methods viable for MATH500. M3 unexpectedly improves MATH500 accuracy above baseline (acc_ret=2.44, likely due to Qwen guidance over-estimating confidence on 100-sample subset). IGSD achieves 2.32x speedup with 88.5% acc retention.

ar_guidance_on_code.finding: M3 is SLOWER than baseline on HumanEval (speedup=0.83x) and adds no accuracy improvement (acc_ret=0). Qwen2.5-0.5B guidance actively hurts throughput on code generation.
ar_guidance_on_code.root_cause: Qwen2.5-0.5B trained on mixed data; code token logits don't align with LLaDA's MASK→token mapping

taxonomy.name: cache_invalidation
taxonomy.impact: Speedup degrades to <1.0x (0.55-0.57x, slower than baseline)
taxonomy.detection: Monitor cache hit rate; alert if < 50%

taxonomy.name: step_starvation
taxonomy.trigger: ANY step_jump (failure occurs at step_jump=2x already)
taxonomy.impact: Accuracy collapse: step_jump=2x acc_ret=0.76 (GSM8K=0.54), step_jump=4x+ acc_ret<0.28
taxonomy.detection: Always check step_jump parameter before deployment; reject M2 for MDMs
taxonomy.remedy: DO NOT use M2 on LLaDA-class MDMs
taxonomy.supporting_data: m2_pareto_full.json: NO_GO verdict, all step_jumps fail accuracy threshold

taxonomy.name: draft_divergence
taxonomy.trigger: combined_accept_rate > 0.85 (tau too low), or T_draft too short (<16)
taxonomy.impact: QAS degrades: tau=0.8 T16 QAS=0.887 vs optimal tau=0.9 T16 QAS=1.194
taxonomy.remedy: Use tau=0.9, T_draft=16 as default operating point
taxonomy.supporting_data: igsd_pareto_full.json: tau=0.9 T_draft=16 achieves best QAS=1.194

taxonomy.name: ar_guidance_conflict
taxonomy.trigger: Combining AR-guided unmasking with speculative decoding in same pipeline
taxonomy.remedy: Use M3 and IGSD independently; prefer M1+IGSD (SYNERGY, Ortho=1.385)
taxonomy.supporting_data: full_pairwise_ortho.json: M3+IGSD ortho=0.493, avg_qas=0.826 (both seeds confirm)

m1_boundary_validation.note: Original atlas had AutoModelForMaskedLM error for M1 boundary probing. Actual M1 data comes from m1_pareto_full.json using LLaDA's native inference with entropy-based cache.

t3_boundary.status: AVOID: higher speedup but QAS drops 37% vs t=2.0

tau=0.8.assessment: FAILING relative to tau=0.9 (lower speedup AND lower QAS)

T_draft=32.assessment: diminishing returns: speedup drops 35% below T16

igsd_stress_characterization.humaneval_behavior: HumanEval pass@1 baseline is near-zero (0.0244); all methods show 0 pass@1. IGSD still provides 1.95x throughput speedup on HumanEval.
igsd_stress_characterization.math500_behavior: IGSD achieves 2.32x speedup with 88.5% accuracy retention on MATH500 (best of all methods)

data_consistency_notes.m2_6x_vs_8x_plateau: step_jump=6x and step_jump=8x produce nearly identical results (speedup 12.363 vs 12.364, acc_ret both 0.243). This is a genuine finding: M2 saturates at 6x jump on LLaDA-8B.
data_consistency_notes.m3_math500_anomaly: M3 shows acc_ret=2.44 on MATH500 (100 samples). Baseline MATH500 accuracy is only 11.07% (11/100), so any slight improvement is magnified. Likely a small-sample artifact.
data_consistency_notes.igsd_qas_ablation_vs_pareto: Ablation igsd_ablation.json reports tau=0.9 T16 QAS=0.956 on 200 GSM8K + 164 HumanEval, while igsd_pareto_full.json reports QAS=1.194 on 1319 GSM8K + 500 MATH500 + 164 HumanEval + 257 MBPP. Different benchmark compositions account for the difference.
data_consistency_notes.previous_atlas_fabrication: Previous atlas had: M2 J=2 speedup=2.1 (actual=3.095), J=4 speedup=5.8 (actual=6.187), J=4 acc_ret=0.51 (actual=0.279), IGSD tau=0.7 QAS=0.82 (actual=1.041), IGSD tau=0.8 QAS=0.95 (actual=0.887). M2 J=6 and J=8 results in old atlas were identical to 3 decimal places which reflected genuine plateau behavior, but the absolute values were wrong.

critical_failure_modes: step_starvation (M2 NO_GO at ALL step_jump values)
safe_configurations: IGSD at tau=0.9, T_draft=16: QAS=1.194, combined_speedup=3.40x
deployment_recommendations: Never deploy M2 on LLaDA-class MDMs (step_starvation is catastrophic at all step_jump values)
Always use M1 threshold >= 2.0 to avoid cache_invalidation overhead
For IGSD: use tau=0.9, T_draft=16 as default (best QAS Pareto point)
Avoid M3+IGSD combination (Ortho=0.493, INTERFERENCE confirmed in both seeds)
Preferred production config: M1+IGSD (QAS=1.654, SYNERGY confirmed, Ortho=1.385)
